heading: Definitions
label: 5727 Definitions
notes: Amendments2010—Par. (20). substituted “plan that defines” for “subordinate plan defines”.Pub. L. 111–275
amendments: Amendments2010—Par. (20). substituted “plan that defines” for “subordinate plan defines”.Pub. L. 111–275

children.heading: .—Availability
children.label: 1 .—Availability
children.text: The term “availability” means ensuring timely and reliable access to and use of information.

children.heading: .—Confidentiality
children.label: 2 .—Confidentiality
children.text: The term “confidentiality” means preserving authorized restrictions on access and disclosure, including means for protecting personal privacy and proprietary information.

children.heading: .—Control techniques
children.label: 3 .—Control techniques
children.text: The term “control techniques” means methods for guiding and controlling the operations of information systems to ensure adherence to the provisions of subchapter III of chapter 35 of title 44 and other related information security requirements.

children.heading: .—Data breach
children.label: 4 .—Data breach
children.text: The term “data breach” means the loss, theft, or other unauthorized access, other than those incidental to the scope of employment, to data containing sensitive personal information, in electronic or printed form, that results in the potential compromise of the confidentiality or integrity of the data.

children.heading: .—Data breach analysis
children.label: 5 .—Data breach analysis
children.text: The term “data breach analysis” means the process used to determine if a data breach has resulted in the misuse of sensitive personal information.

children.heading: .—Fraud resolution systems
children.label: 6 .—Fraud resolution systems
children.text: The term “fraud resolution services” means services to assist an individual in the process of recovering and rehabilitating the credit of the individual after the individual experiences identity theft.

children.heading: .—Identity theft
children.label: 7 .—Identity theft
children.text: The term “identity theft” has the meaning given such term under section 603 of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ().15 U.S.C. 1681a

children.heading: .—Identity theft insurance
children.label: 8 .—Identity theft insurance
children.text: The term “identity theft insurance” means any insurance policy that pays benefits for costs, including travel costs, notary fees, and postage costs, lost wages, and legal fees and expenses associated with efforts to correct and ameliorate the effects and results of identity theft of the insured individual.

children.heading: .—Information owner
children.label: 9 .—Information owner
children.text: The term “information owner” means an agency official with statutory or operational authority for specified information and responsibility for establishing the criteria for its creation, collection, processing, dissemination, or disposal, which responsibilities may extend to interconnected systems or groups of interconnected systems.

children.heading: .—Information resources
children.label: 10 .—Information resources
children.text: The term “information resources” means information in any medium or form and its related resources, such as personnel, equipment, funds, and information technology.

children.heading: .—Information security
children.label: 11 .—Information security
children.text: The term “information security” means protecting information and information systems from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction in order to provide integrity, confidentiality, and availability.

children.heading: .—Information security requirements
children.label: 12 .—Information security requirements
children.text: The term “information security requirements” means information security requirements promulgated in accordance with law, or directed by the Secretary of Commerce, the National Institute of Standards and Technology, and the Office of Management and Budget, and, as to national security systems, the President.

children.heading: .—Information system
children.label: 13 .—Information system
children.text: The term “information system” means a discrete set of information resources organized for the collection, processing, maintenance, use, sharing, dissemination, or disposition of information, whether automated or manual.

children.heading: .—Integrity
children.label: 14 .—Integrity
children.text: The term “integrity” means guarding against improper information modification or destruction, and includes ensuring information non-repudiation and authenticity.

children.heading: .—National security system
children.label: 15 .—National security system
children.text: The term “national security system” means an information system that is protected at all times by policies and procedures established for the processing, maintenance, use, sharing, dissemination or disposition of information that has been specifically authorized under criteria established by statute or Executive Order to be kept classified in the interest of national defense or foreign policy.

children.label: A
children.text: A description of the security weakness.

children.label: B
children.text: The identity of the office or organization responsible for resolving the weakness.

children.label: C
children.text: An estimate of resources required to resolve the weakness by fiscal year.

children.label: D
children.text: The scheduled completion date.

children.label: E
children.text: Key milestones with estimated completion dates.

children.label: F
children.text: Any changes to the original key milestone date.

children.label: G
children.text: The source that identified the weakness.

children.label: H
children.text: The status of efforts to correct the weakness.

children.heading: .—Principal credit reporting agency
children.label: 17 .—Principal credit reporting agency
children.text: The term “principal credit reporting agency” means a consumer reporting agency as described in section 603(p) of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ().15 U.S.C. 1681a(p)

children.heading: .—Security incident
children.label: 18 .—Security incident
children.text: The term “security incident” means an event that has, or could have, resulted in loss or damage to Department assets, or sensitive information, or an action that breaches Department security procedures.

children.label: A
children.text: Education, financial transactions, medical history, and criminal or employment history.

children.label: B
children.text: Information that can be used to distinguish or trace the individual’s identity, including name, social security number, date and place of birth, mother’s maiden name, or biometric records.

children.heading: .—Subordinate plan
children.label: 20 .—Subordinate plan
children.text: The term “subordinate plan”, also referred to as a “system security plan”, means a plan that defines the security controls that are either planned or implemented for networks, facilities, systems, or groups of systems, as appropriate, within a specific accreditation boundary.

children.heading: .—Training
children.label: 21 .—Training
children.text: The term “training” means a learning experience in which an individual is taught to execute a specific information security procedure or understand the information security common body of knowledge.

children.heading: .—Va national rules of behavior
children.label: 22 .—Va national rules of behavior
children.text: The term “VA National Rules of Behavior” means a set of Department rules that describes the responsibilities and expected behavior of personnel with regard to information system usage.

children.heading: .—Va sensitive data
children.label: 23 .—Va sensitive data
children.text: The term “VA sensitive data” means all Department data, on any storage media or in any form or format, which requires protection due to the risk of harm that could result from inadvertent or deliberate disclosure, alteration, or destruction of the information and includes information whose improper use or disclosure could adversely affect the ability of an agency to accomplish its mission, proprietary information, and records about individuals requiring protection under applicable confidentiality provisions.
